name: ws05: Email channel — implement IMAP poll loop and SMTP outbound send
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Stubbed runtime table; Task List #4)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/email/channel.rs:166-217
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/06-channel-enhancements/02-phase-EEnterprise-email-gchat-teams.md

## Problem / gap
The Email `ChannelAdapter` ships with a passing test suite but the runtime is a stub. `start()` polls with `debug!("polling for new emails (stub)")` and never opens an IMAP connection; `send()` fabricates a synthetic `<ts-target@host>` Message-ID without invoking SMTP. With `--features email`, the gateway will start cleanly, accept outbound `send()` calls, and silently drop every message. The Element-06 tracker reports this as complete; it is not.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `start()` opens an IMAP connection (using `imap` crate behind the `email` feature), polls INBOX with IDLE or interval-based fetch, parses RFC822 messages, and emits real `InboundMessage`/`MessagePayload` events to the host
- [ ] `send()` constructs a MIME message via `lettre` and submits to SMTP with TLS; the returned message id is the real Message-ID returned by the server
- [ ] Auth supports both `password`/`password_env` and (at minimum) STARTTLS + implicit TLS
- [ ] Reconnect-with-backoff on transport errors; cancellation respected
- [ ] Unit + integration tests against a mock IMAP/SMTP harness (or an embedded test server)
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` and `scripts/build.sh clippy --features email` pass
- [ ] `04-element-06-tracker.md` updated to reflect real I/O landed (no longer "stub IMAP/SMTP runtime")

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features email`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
E2 was tracked under E-Enterprise. The `imap` and `lettre` deps are not yet in `clawft-channels/Cargo.toml`. Channel-stub WARN headers and `tracing::warn!` on start() landed in this session (documentation half); this item is the real I/O implementation.

name: ws05: Google Chat channel — implement Pub/Sub subscribe and chat.spaces.messages.create
description_md:
## Source

- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/06-channel-enhancements/02-phase-EEnterprise-email-gchat-teams.md

## Problem / gap
Google Chat adapter is a skeleton: `start()` does not subscribe to Pub/Sub events; `send()` builds a synthetic `spaces/{target}/messages/gchat-{ts}` id without POSTing to the Google Chat REST API. The track was originally blocked on F6 (OAuth2); F6 has now landed in `clawft-plugin-oauth2`, removing the blocker.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] OAuth2 / service-account credentials wired through `clawft-plugin-oauth2`
- [ ] Pub/Sub subscription receives space/message events and routes them to `MessagePayload` (or a polling fallback if Pub/Sub is not configured)
- [ ] `send()` POSTs to `chat.spaces.messages.create` and returns the real `name` field from the response
- [ ] Bot self-message suppression and basic rate-limit handling
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features google-chat` passes; integration test against a mocked Google API
- [ ] Tracker entry updated to show real I/O landed

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features google-chat`
- blocked-by: F6 OAuth2 (already landed)

## Notes
E5a in the SPARC tracker. Channel-stub WARN headers landed in this session; this is the real implementation work.

name: ws05: Microsoft Teams channel — implement Bot Framework auth and Graph message POST
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Stubbed runtime table; Task List #7)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/teams/channel.rs:88-135
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/06-channel-enhancements/02-phase-EEnterprise-email-gchat-teams.md

## Problem / gap
Teams adapter does not register with the Bot Framework, never acquires an Azure AD client-credentials token, and `send()` does not POST to `/teams/{team-id}/channels/{channel-id}/messages` via Graph. With `--features teams`, the gateway will validate config and silently drop every outbound message.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Azure AD client-credentials token acquisition with refresh-on-expiry
- [ ] Bot Framework webhook receiver parses `Activity` JSON into `MessagePayload`
- [ ] Outbound POST to Graph `/teams/{team-id}/channels/{channel-id}/messages` returns real message id
- [ ] Reconnect/backoff on auth failures; secrets routed through `SecretRef`
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features teams` passes; integration test against a mocked Graph API
- [ ] Tracker entry updated to show real I/O landed

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features teams`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
E5b in the SPARC tracker. Bot Framework registration step needs an external bot registration; document that as part of channel onboarding.

name: ws05: WhatsApp channel — implement Cloud API webhook receiver and outbound POST
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Stubbed runtime table; Task List #2)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/whatsapp/channel.rs:74-105
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/06-channel-enhancements/03-phase-EConsumer-whatsapp-signal-matrix.md

## Problem / gap
WhatsApp Cloud API webhook listener is missing; `send()` fabricates a `wamid.{ts}` id without POSTing to `/v18.0/{phone_number_id}/messages`. There is no `X-Hub-Signature-256` verification, and the E3 risk register's 429 backoff handler does not exist yet. Combination of silent-drop on send and no signature verification makes this a security-relevant gap.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Webhook receiver verifies `X-Hub-Signature-256` HMAC against the app secret before processing any payload
- [ ] Inbound webhook events parsed into `MessagePayload`
- [ ] `send()` POSTs to `/v{api}/{phone_number_id}/messages` and returns the real `wamid.*` id from the response
- [ ] 429 / rate-limit backoff respecting `Retry-After`
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features whatsapp` passes; integration test against a mocked Cloud API; signature-verify unit tests with positive + negative cases
- [ ] Tracker entry updated

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features whatsapp`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
E3 in the SPARC tracker. Signature verification MUST land before the receiver accepts any payload.

name: ws05: Signal channel — spawn signal-cli daemon and wire JSON-RPC reader
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Stubbed runtime table; Task List #3)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/signal/channel.rs:90-148
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/06-channel-enhancements/03-phase-EConsumer-whatsapp-signal-matrix.md

## Problem / gap
Signal adapter never spawns `signal-cli daemon` as a subprocess. `start()` does not establish a JSON-RPC channel; `send()` does not invoke `tokio::process::Command` even though argument sanitization runs. There is no PID tracking and no auto-restart on crash. With `--features signal`, the gateway accepts outbound calls and silently drops every message.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `start()` spawns `signal-cli daemon` with sanitized args and an mpsc reader on stdout/stderr (JSON-RPC)
- [ ] PID tracking with `Child::id()`; auto-restart on crash with exponential backoff and a kill-timeout for hung children
- [ ] Inbound JSON-RPC notifications parsed into `MessagePayload`
- [ ] `send()` issues a JSON-RPC `send` call to the daemon and returns the real `timestamp`/message id
- [ ] Cancellation cleanly terminates the daemon (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout)
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features signal` passes; integration test using a mock JSON-RPC peer
- [ ] Tracker entry updated

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features signal`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
E4 in the SPARC tracker. Argument sanitization already lands; this item is the spawn + lifecycle + I/O glue.

name: ws05: Matrix channel — implement /sync long-poll, room auto-join, and m.room.message send
description_md:

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Auto-join rooms in the configured allow-list; parse `m.room.message` events into `MessagePayload`
- [ ] `send()` issues `PUT /_matrix/client/v3/rooms/{room}/send/m.room.message/{txn}` with idempotent transaction id and returns the real event id
- [ ] Cancellation respected; `since` token persisted to disk for resume
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features matrix` passes; integration test against a mocked Matrix server
- [ ] Tracker entry updated

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production deployment of `--features matrix`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
E5 in the SPARC tracker. `since`-token persistence avoids replaying the full timeline on every restart.

name: ws05: IRC channel — implement TCP/TLS dial, CAP/NICK/USER, JOIN, and PRIVMSG read/write
description_md:
## Source

- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/irc/channel.rs:82, 128 (TODO markers)

## Problem / gap
IRC adapter is the only in-channels-crate file with explicit `// TODO` markers. The entire start loop is a log-only stub; `auth_method`, `reconnect_delay_secs`, channel JOINs, and PRIVMSG read/write are unimplemented. `send()` returns `irc-{target}-{ts}` without ever opening a socket. The `irc` crate is not yet a dependency.

- [ ] TCP + TLS dial; CAP/NICK/USER negotiation; SASL or NickServ auth via `auth_method`
- [ ] JOIN configured channels; PRIVMSG reader emits `MessagePayload`; `send()` emits PRIVMSG and returns real message id (or sequence id when server echoes are unavailable)
- [ ] Reconnect with `reconnect_delay_secs` backoff
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features irc` passes; integration test against a small embedded IRC server
- [ ] Replace the two TODO markers; tracker entry updated

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes
This is the most explicit stub of the seven adapters; the `// TODO` markers themselves should be removed when the work lands.

name: ws05: Discord — fix edit_message rate-limit handling to actually sleep
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Open question 6; Task List #8)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/discord/api.rs:153-161 (edit_message)

## Problem / gap
`DiscordApiClient::edit_message` reads rate-limit headers and emits a warning log but does not actually `tokio::time::sleep` before returning. This contrasts with `create_message`, which does sleep. Likely a copy-paste oversight; under sustained edit traffic the client will hammer Discord into 429s.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `edit_message` honours `x-ratelimit-remaining` / `-reset-after` and sleeps before returning, identical to `create_message`
- [ ] Unit test that asserts the sleep path fires when remaining=0
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: none
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
Small fix; bundle with discord-chunker work if convenient.

name: ws05: Permissions — emit allow_from_match metadata in Slack and Telegram channels
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Open question 10; Task List #10)
- code: Slack and Telegram channel factories / inbound builders

## Problem / gap
Discord emits an `allow_from_match` metadata flag that `PermissionResolver` uses to promote an allowed sender out of the zero-trust tier. Slack and Telegram do not. A user listed in `slack.dm.allow_from` is passed through to the pipeline but stays at zero-trust. Behaviour asymmetry between channels is a footgun.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Slack inbound emits `allow_from_match=true` on senders matching `dm.allow_from` / `group.allow_from`
- [ ] Telegram inbound emits `allow_from_match=true` on senders matching `allowed_users`
- [ ] Unit tests exercise both channels' promotion path through `PermissionResolver`
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: none
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
Keep the metadata key identical to Discord's so the resolver code does not need a per-channel branch.

name: ws05: Web channel — gate is_allowed on D-7 auth middleware enablement
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Open question 5; Task List #23)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/web/channel.rs (`is_allowed`)
- prior planning: .planning/development_notes/05-axum-api-layer/decisions.md (D-7)

## Problem / gap
`WebChannel::is_allowed` returns `true` unconditionally because "auth is handled by the API middleware, not here." With D-7 (auth middleware) still off, *nothing* authenticates web-channel inbound. This is not documented as a known gap, and any non-localhost gateway deployment is silently open.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either: enable D-7 auth middleware before exposing web channel publicly, OR refuse to start web channel when auth middleware is not wired and `bind_addr` is non-loopback
- [ ] `is_allowed` carries a doc comment explaining the dependency on auth middleware
- [ ] `docs/guides/channels.md` (or equivalent) calls out the open-by-default behaviour and the D-7 dependency
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: any non-localhost gateway deployment with `web` channel
- blocked-by: D-7 auth middleware enablement (separate item)

## Notes
Docs+behaviour gate is acceptable for 0.7.x; full D-7 enablement is a larger UI-dependent change.

name: ws05: Voice channel — replace stub start/send with real capture+VAD+STT and TTS playback
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Task List #21; cross-ref to ws10)
- code: crates/clawft-plugin/src/voice/channel.rs
- prior planning: .planning/development_notes/step3-vs1.3-voice-channel.md

## Problem / gap
`VoiceChannel` ships an `ChannelAdapter` impl but `start()` simply waits for cancellation, `send()` logs TTS text without playing it, and `deliver_inbound` from real transcriptions is deferred. CLI `weft voice talk` shows status only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `start()` runs a real capture + VAD + STT loop and calls `deliver_inbound` with real transcripts
- [ ] `send()` synthesises TTS via the configured provider and plays the audio
- [ ] Cancellation aborts both loops cleanly
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features voice,voice-stt,voice-tts` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: any honest "voice MVP" claim for 0.7.x
- blocked-by: ws10 STT/TTS canonical-path ADR; sherpa-rs/cpal Cargo deps

## Notes
This adapter is in `clawft-plugin/voice/`, not `clawft-channels/`, but the audit places it in scope because it is an inbound surface. Coordinate with ws10 SC-1/SC-4 work.

name: ws05: Gateway — wire Axum auth middleware (D-7) and gate non-public routes
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Open question 2; Task List #13)
- code: crates/clawft-services/src/api/{auth.rs,mod.rs} (TokenStore + auth_middleware exist; not wired)
- prior planning: .planning/development_notes/05-axum-api-layer/decisions.md (D-7)

## Problem / gap
`TokenStore` and `auth_middleware` exist in the api crate but are intentionally not enabled in the router. Today every gateway deployment outside localhost is unauthenticated. This is the single largest 0.7.0 release-gate security exposure on the gateway side.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `auth_middleware` wired into the router, gating every non-`/api/auth/token` and non-`/api/health` route
- [ ] `TokenStore` with at-rest 0600 file persistence; bootstrap token-issue flow documented
- [ ] Localhost / dev-mode bypass behind an explicit config flag, off by default
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes; e2e test exercises a 401 on missing token and 200 on a valid one
- [ ] `docs/guides/` updated; D-7 marked enabled in decisions.md

## Dependencies
- blocks: WebChannel hardening; rate limiting; CSP rollout
- blocked-by: minimum UI login surface (or accept a CLI-issued token-only flow for 0.7.x)

## Notes
UI login page is a larger story; consider shipping CLI-token-only auth for 0.7.0 and deferring UI login.

name: ws05: Discord chunker — preserve fenced code, balance markdown, support Nitro/embeds/file fallback
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Deferred / orphaned items: Discord chunking improvements; Task List #9)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/discord/channel.rs (chunk_message)

## Problem / gap
The current chunker handles newline / space / hard-split at 2000 chars but does not: preserve fenced code blocks across splits (mid-block split renders as two malformed half-blocks); re-balance markdown emphasis tokens (`**bold**` opened in chunk 1 not auto-closed/reopened); detect Nitro/boost 4000-char limits; support embeds (6000-char total / 25-field limits); fall back to file upload past N chunks.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Code-fence preservation: when split lands inside ```fence```, close and reopen the fence with the same language tag
- [ ] Markdown emphasis re-balance for `*`, `_`, `**`, `__`
- [ ] Nitro 4000-char detection (config or runtime via `User`)
- [ ] Embed support (`content` + `embeds[]`) with field/title/total limits
- [ ] File-upload fallback past configurable N chunks
- [ ] Unit tests for each split scenario
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: none
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
Called out in MEMORY.md project context. Defer to 0.8.x — current chunker is correct in raw byte terms; this is quality-of-output polish.

name: ws05: PluginHost C7 unification — migrate Telegram/Discord/Slack to ChannelAdapter
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 05-channels.md (Deferred items: PluginHost C7 unification; Task List #11)
- code: crates/clawft-channels/src/plugin_host.rs (ChannelAdapterShim, ChannelAdapterHostBridge)
- prior planning: Element 04 / C7

## Problem / gap
The trait shim (`ChannelAdapterShim`) exists, but Telegram, Discord, Slack still implement the legacy `Channel` trait directly. Migrating them to `ChannelAdapter` is the C7 deliverable; the migration changes the cancellation contract (poll-based plugin token vs `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`) and the inbound payload type.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Telegram, Discord, Slack reimplemented on `ChannelAdapter`
- [ ] Decision: keep or retire `ChannelAdapterShim` (with a deprecation note if retained for third-party plugins)
- [ ] `register_command` migration plan resolved (see separate item)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass; new tests cover the migrated cancellation path
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` and `clippy` pass

## Dependencies
- blocks: full PluginHost simplification
- blocked-by: slash-command surface decision

## Notes
Affects every plugin-host call site. 0.8.x because it is invasive but not a broken-claim issue.

name: ws05: Slash-command surface — decide consumer for ChannelHost::register_command

name: ws05: Telegram — document or remove redundant 1s poll-interval sleep

name: ws05: WeftOS white-label — add brand() accessor and remove hard-coded clawft strings
description_md:

name: ws07: AgentRouter — wire routing into MessageBus inbound dispatch
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 07-multi-agent-routing.md (Status & Timeline L-Routing row; Deferred item #4; Task List)
- code: crates/clawft-core/src/agent_routing.rs (router type + tests); crates/clawft-core/src/bootstrap.rs:89, 341-355 (router stored on AgentContext); crates/clawft-core/src/agent/loop_core.rs (no router consultation)
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/09-multi-agent-routing/02-phase-LRouting-agents-swarming.md

## Problem / gap
The element-09 tracker reports "14/14 done", but only the type-level scaffolding shipped. `AgentRouter` is constructed and stored on `AgentContext`, yet `MessageBus::consume_inbound` and `AgentLoop::run` never call `router.route(&msg)`. A single agent loop still processes every message; per-user agent routing is not observable end-to-end. The L1 deliverable is feature-flagged on but inert.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `MessageBus::consume_inbound` (or `AgentLoop::run`) calls `AgentRouter::route` and either dispatches to a per-agent runtime or carries the routed `agent_id` through the pipeline
- [ ] Default catch-all route preserved when no routes match
- [ ] Integration test: two configured agents, two senders, asserts each lands on the routed agent loop / agent_id field
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes
- [ ] element-09 tracker updated to drop the false 14/14 claim or mark this row in-progress until the wiring lands

## Dependencies
- blocks: AgentRuntime (L2); AgentBus integration; per-agent MCP override
- blocked-by: design decision on "multiple AgentLoops per agent" vs "one loop, per-message agent runtime" (open question in audit)

## Notes
This is one of the audit's top-10 cross-cutting risks. Without it, the L-Routing track is type-only.

The file `clawft-services/src/delegation/flow.rs` does not exist. `flow_available` was removed from the engine; `resolve_availability` collapses `Flow` -> `Claude`. The `Flow` `DelegationTarget` variant and `claude_flow_enabled` config field still exist, unused. Users with `claude_flow_enabled = true` silently re-route to Claude.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded (ADR or decisions.md): keep FlowDelegator OR retire it
- [ ] If keep: implement `delegation/flow.rs` per the M1 spec (subprocess spawn with `env_clear()`, `CLAWFT_DELEGATION_DEPTH` threading, timeout + `child.kill()`, `flow_available` runtime detection via `which::which("claude")` cached on `AtomicBool`)

## Notes
Most likely outcome (per audit): retire. Either way, the tracker must stop claiming Done.

name: ws07: Recursive-delegation guard — thread depth via CLAWFT_DELEGATION_DEPTH and enforce MAX=3
description_md:
## Source

- code: crates/clawft-services/src/mcp/bridge.rs (pseudocode `MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH = 3` not landed); crates/clawft-services/src/delegation/claude.rs (no depth threading)
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/phase4/09-multi-agent-routing/01-phase-MFoundation-flow-delegator.md

## Problem / gap
Recursive `delegate_task` -> Claude -> MCP-bridge -> `delegate_task` is currently unbounded. `CLAWFT_DELEGATION_DEPTH` is not threaded; `MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH = 3` from the bridge pseudocode never landed. Cost and latency blow up under accidental recursion.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Depth carried on each delegation attempt (env var on subprocess paths; tool-call metadata on in-process paths to keep WASM compatibility per audit open question)
- [ ] Hard cap at 3 (configurable). Beyond cap: `DelegationError::FallbackExhausted` with a clear message
- [ ] Tests exercising the cap and the increment path
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: McpBridge real connection (call_claude_tool_with_depth)
- blocked-by: env-var vs metadata decision (open question in audit)

## Notes
Must land before any production deployment of the McpBridge real connection.

name: ws07: McpServerManager — implement drain-and-swap on remove_server
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 07-multi-agent-routing.md (TODO table `mcp/discovery.rs:138-139`; Deferred item #9; Task List)
- code: crates/clawft-services/src/mcp/discovery.rs:138-139 (self-documenting stub: "in a full implementation, in-flight calls would be completed before disconnection")

## Problem / gap
`remove_server` only marks the server `Draining` and removes it from the map. No `AtomicU32` in-flight counter, no `InFlightGuard`, no 30s wait, no transport drop, no `call_tool` routing through the manager. The current behaviour can sever an in-flight tool call mid-stream.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `inflight: AtomicU32` on `ManagedMcpServer`; `InFlightGuard` increments/decrements around `call_tool`
- [ ] `remove_server` sets state to `Draining`, waits up to 30s for `inflight == 0`, then drops the transport
- [ ] `call_tool` routes through the manager and respects `Draining` (rejects new calls, completes in-flight)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests: drain completes cleanly under no traffic; rejects new calls under `Draining`; force-drops after 30s if hung
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes
- [ ] Replace the inline "in a full implementation" comment with the real impl

## Dependencies
- blocks: hot-reload watcher; CLI `weft mcp remove`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
One of the most explicit self-documenting stubs in the codebase.

name: ws07: McpServerManager — add transport factory and url/command/tempfile validators
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 07-multi-agent-routing.md (Deferred item #10; Task List)
- prior planning: M-Advanced 5.4 (security)

## Problem / gap
No stdio-vs-http transport factory; no `validate_mcp_url` (SSRF guard); no `validate_command_path` (shell metachar guard); no `tempfile`-with-`0600` for MCP temp files. Anyone able to write to clawft.toml's `[tools.mcp_servers]` can today launch arbitrary commands or hit arbitrary URLs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Transport factory dispatching `stdio` vs `http` based on config
- [ ] `validate_mcp_url` rejects loopback bypass tricks, file://, non-allowlisted hosts (configurable)
- [ ] `validate_command_path` rejects shell metachars, relative paths outside an allow-listed directory
- [ ] MCP temp files created with mode 0600 via `tempfile`
- [ ] Unit tests with positive and negative cases for each validator
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: hot-reload watcher (must validate on diff)
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
Security-relevant; do not ship hot-reload without these.

name: ws07: weft mcp-server — implement allowed_tools + CommandPolicy/UrlPolicy on tools/call (3H CRIT-01)
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 07-multi-agent-routing.md (Deferred item #14; Task List CRIT-01)

## Problem / gap
`weft mcp-server` `tools/call` dispatches directly to `tool_registry.execute` with no `allowed_tools` allow-list, no `CommandPolicy`, no `UrlPolicy`. An external MCP client connecting to weft can invoke any registered tool, including shell and HTTP tools, with zero policy enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `allowed_tools` config in clawft.toml (defaults to a conservative read-only set)
- [ ] `CommandPolicy` (binary allow-list, arg pattern checks) enforced on shell-like tools
- [ ] `UrlPolicy` (host allow-list, scheme allow-list) enforced on HTTP tools
- [ ] `tools/call` rejects with -32601 / a clear error code when the requested tool is outside the allow-list or fails policy
- [ ] Unit tests for each policy
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: any production exposure of `weft mcp-server`
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
3H CRIT-01. The single highest-risk issue in the MCP server-mode surface.

name: ws07: delegation_config — reconcile claude_enabled defaults across serde-default vs Default::default
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 07-multi-agent-routing.md (Dead-code risk; Task List)
- code: crates/clawft-types/src/delegation.rs:163-173 (`delegation_config_from_empty_json` asserts `claude_enabled == false`)

## Problem / gap
`delegation_config_from_empty_json` asserts `claude_enabled == false` for `{}` because `serde(default)` uses `bool::default()` (false) rather than `Default::default()` (true). The two tests are technically consistent but the divergence is a documented footgun.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either: change the serde default to derive from `Default::default()` (so `{}` produces `claude_enabled = true`), OR document the divergence in a doc comment + user-facing docs
- [ ] Tests updated to match the chosen behaviour
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: none
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
User-facing footgun; pick one and document it.

name: ws10: Voice STT — record ADR choosing in-process sherpa-rs vs substrate clawft-service-whisper
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 10-voice.md (Open questions: Two voice STT stacks coexist; P0 Task #1)
- code: crates/clawft-plugin/src/voice/stt.rs (in-process sherpa-rs plan, all stubbed); crates/clawft-service-whisper/src/* (real working substrate pipeline)
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/voice/00-orchestrator.md; .planning/sensors/PIPELINE-PRIMITIVE-SPIKE.md

## Problem / gap
Two unrelated voice-STT stacks coexist with no ADR reconciling them. `clawft-plugin/voice/*` plans in-process sherpa-rs (all stubs). `clawft-service-whisper` is a real, working substrate-side whisper.cpp HTTP pipeline. The SPARC voice plan does not mention the substrate pipeline; the substrate journal explicitly chose HTTP over whisper-rs FFI. Which is the canonical Talk-Mode STT for 0.7.0+? Does `VoiceChannel` consume substrate transcript events, or own its own recognizer?

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ADR in `docs/adr/` decides the canonical voice-STT path and the relationship between the two surfaces
- [ ] If unified: explicit deletion or rebrand of the losing path
- [ ] If split: named contracts (e.g. `VoiceChannel` consumes substrate transcripts on `substrate/_derived/transcript/<src>/mic`)
- [ ] Documented join key between `TranscriptLogger` JSONL session_key and substrate `<source-node-id>`
- [ ] orchestrator-log.md and W-VOICE plan docs cross-reference the ADR

## Dependencies
- blocks: every other ws10 implementation item (cannot wire a path that has not been chosen)
- blocked-by: none

## Notes
This is the foundational decision for ws10 0.7.x work.

name: ws10: SC-4 voice permission flags — gate voice-triggered tool execution by Level 0/1/2

Voice-specific permission flags (`voice_listen`, `voice_speak`, `wake_word`, `talk_mode`, `voice_exec_shell`, `voice_delegate`) are not implemented. - [ ] Destructive actions (write tools, shell, delegation) are gated and require an explicit voice-confirmation prompt when the request originates from voice
- [ ] `VoiceCommand.confirm` consumed by the registry to drive confirmation flow

name: ws10: SC-7 model integrity — replace placeholder SHA-256 and add Ed25519 manifest verify
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 10-voice.md (TODOs in source: models.rs:54,66,78; Open questions: Model SHA-256 hashes are placeholders; P0 Task #3; SC-7)
- code: crates/clawft-plugin/src/voice/models.rs:54,66,78 (`PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_*`)
- prior planning: .planning/sparc/voice/06-voice-security-review.md (SC-7)

## Problem / gap
SHA-256 hashes for STT/TTS/VAD models in `models.rs` are `PLACEHOLDER_SHA256_*`. The download manager will silently accept tampered models because it only checks file existence. Ed25519 signed-manifest verification is not implemented. P0 security gap hiding behind a stub.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Real SHA-256 hashes for the canonical model versions (or a build-time generator that produces them at release time)
- [ ] `is_cached` refuses to load a model whose hash does not match (or whose hash is a placeholder)
- [ ] Ed25519-signed model manifest verified before any model is loaded
- [ ] Tests: tampered file rejected; missing manifest rejected; valid model accepted
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features voice` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: any real model download path; `weft voice setup`
- blocked-by: STT/TTS canonical-path ADR

## Notes
Release-gate blocker per audit. P0 security control.

name: ws10: EchoCanceller and NoiseSuppressor — replace deceptive passthroughs with real DSP
description_md:
## Source
- audit: 10-voice.md (P1 Task #12; orphaned: EchoCanceller.feed_reference unused; NoiseSuppressor.noise_floor unused; doc-comment markers in echo.rs:88, noise.rs:68)
- code: crates/clawft-plugin/src/voice/echo.rs; crates/clawft-plugin/src/voice/noise.rs

## Problem / gap
Both modules are passthroughs. EchoCanceller has a circular reference buffer that `process()` ignores; NoiseSuppressor tracks RMS noise floor via EMA but does not filter. The presence of computed-but-unused state is misleading.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] EchoCanceller: NLMS or frequency-domain adaptive filter consuming the reference buffer
- [ ] NoiseSuppressor: spectral subtraction or RNNoise consuming `noise_floor`
- [ ] Either implementation, OR explicit removal of the dead reference state and a doc comment marking the modules as no-op-by-design
- [ ] Tests against fixture audio with known noise/echo
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh test --features voice` passes

## Dependencies
- blocks: real Talk Mode quality
- blocked-by: cpal capture path

## Notes
Defer to 0.8.x.

description_md:

Multiple orphan surfaces: `WakeWordEvent::Error` emitted nowhere; `VoiceStatus::Transcribing` defined but never reached; `voice-chat.ts::sendVoiceMessage` not invoked by any UI component; `WakeConfig.model_path: Option<String>` vs `WakeWordConfig.model_path: PathBuf` divergence with no bridge; `VoicePersonality.greeting_prefix` and `VoiceCommand.confirm` defined but not consumed (latter is covered separately by SC-4 / SC-6 work).
